title: Race-Year Grouped Validation
summary: Random holdout likely leaks near-identical race-driver-stint trajectories across train and validation, inflating confidence in features that memorize local sequence structure.
rationale: Random holdout likely leaks near-identical race-driver-stint trajectories across train and validation, inflating confidence in features that memorize local sequence structure. A race/year grouped split better matches unseen-event generalization and can reveal which features survive public LB.
implementation_hint: Evaluate with GroupKFold or holdout by Year/Race, optionally rolling-origin by Year. Keep the same feature set/model, compare random CV vs race-group CV, then train final on all rows only after choosing features by grouped CV.
expected_effect: May not directly raise local random CV, but should reduce validation traps and improve public/private correlation.
risk: If Kaggle test rows are from the same races/distribution as train, grouped CV may be too pessimistic and discard useful memorization.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
